study_scheme:
Linguistics Linguistics Applicable to students admitted in 2025-26
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 63 units of
courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
LING1000# and any two courses from:
ANTH1020, ARTS1005,1010,
BMBL1001, CHES1101 or 1102, CHLL1900
or 1902 or 1903, CURE1000 or 1400, 1110,ENGE1000, FAAS1900,
HIST1000 or 1700 or 1701, JASP1090,
MUSC1000, PHIL1110,
THEO1000, TRAN1000
2.  |  Required Courses:
(a)  |  LING1001#[a] or 1002#[a]  |  3
(b)  |  LING1003#, 2003,
2004, 2005, 2006, 2007, 2008,  |  21
Research
Components/ Capstone Courses/ Final Year Project:
(c)  |  LING4000, 4001  |  6
3.  |  Elective Courses:
(a)  |  Six courses from the 3 Areas,
with at least one course from each
area, excluding LING3405 and 3406.  |  18
(b)  |  Language courses
to enhance students’ exposure to other language(s):  |  6
6 units of language courses (from one to two languages) other than Chinese (Cantonese/Putonghua) and English,
and excluding the 1-unit modern language courses, i.e. FREN1001, GERM1001, HKSL1001, KORE1001,
and SPAN1001.
Total:  |  63
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  LING courses
at 2000 and above level as well as those labeled as # will be included in
the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Students may be required to
participate in activities such as field trips and study tours that foster
their learning of linguistics. Students are responsible for part of or
all costs incurred in relation to these activities.
3.  |  Students whose first language is not
Chinese may take Cantonese and Putonghua as language courses for 3(b).
4.  |  A Linguistics Major student who
pursues a second Major in Chinese Language and Literature can be exempted
from taking CHLL3162, 4161 and 4110 by taking LING1002, 3108 and 4401 respectively. Units earned
can be counted towards both Major Programmes.
5.  |  A Linguistics Major student who
pursues a second Major in Psychology can be exempted from taking PSYC3430
by taking LING3202. Units earned can be counted towards both Major
Programmes.
6.  |  A Linguistics Major student who
pursues a second Major in Translation can be exempted
from taking TRAN2610 by
taking LING3401. Units
earned can be counted towards both Major Programmes.
[a]  |  The course not taken to fulfill
requirement 2(a) may be
taken as a free elective course.

Linguistics and Translation Double Major Programme
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 90 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package  |  9
LING1000# and any two courses from: ANTH1020,
ARTS1005,1010, BMBL1001[a], CHES1101 or 1102, CHLL1900 or 1902 or 1903, CURE1000 or 1400, 1110,
ENGE1000, FAAS1900 HIST1000 or 1700 or 1701, JASP1090, MUSC1000,
PHIL1110, THEO1000
2.  |  Required Courses:
1st Major: Linguistics
(a)  |  LING1001#[b] or 1002#[b], 1003#, 2003, 2004, 2005, 2006, 2007,
2008  |  24
2nd Major: Translation
(b)  |  TRAN1010, 1030, 2070#, 2610#  |  12
3.  |  Elective Courses:
1st Major: Linguistics  |  12
(a)  |  Four courses chosen from the 3 Areas below, with at least one
course from each area:
(i)  |  Core Linguistics BMBL2001#, 2002#, 2003#, LING3101, 3102, 3103, 3104, 3107,
3108, 4401
(ii)  |  Language and Mind BMBL2004#, 2005# LING3201, 3202, 3203, 3204, 3206, 3207, 4201,
4202
(iii)  |  Language and Culture, and Linguistic Applications BMBL1001[a], 1002#, 3002#, 3003# LING2301, 3208, 3401, 3403,
4402
2nd Major: Translation
(b)  |  Students
are required to complete 27 units of elective courses from the following
lists of courses, including at least one course from each of Electives
Cluster A and Electives Cluster B:  |  27
Electives Cluster A:
Translation Studies TRAN2120,3080, 3720, 4070,
4210
Electives Cluster B:
Interpreting TRAN2140, 2150, 2240,
2250, 3240, 4050
Other electives TRAN1120, 1130, 2020,
2050, 2110, 2130, 2840, 3170, 3180, 3270, 3280, 3620, 3800, 3810, 3820,
3830, 3844, 3845, 3850, 3860, 3870, 3880, 3890, 4800, 4840 and unselected
courses from Clusters A and B.
4.  |  Research Component/ Capstone Course/ Final
Year Project:  |  6
LING4000 and 4001
Total:  |  90
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  LING courses at 2000 and above level as well as those labeled
as # will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours
classification.
2.  |  Students may be required to participate in activities such as
field trips and study tours that foster their learning of linguistics.
Students are responsible for part of or all costs
incurred in relation to these activities.
3.  |  Students are encouraged to learn a modern language or take
courses in a discipline relevant to a translation area in which the student
would like to specialize after graduation using their free electives.
[a]  |  If the course is not taken to fulfill requirement 1, it may be
taken as an elective course for fulfillment of requirement 3(a)(iii).
[b]  |  The
course not taken to fulfill requirement 2(a) may be taken as a free elective course.

as well as those labeled as # will be included in the calculation of

2.  |  Students may be required to participate in activities such as
field trips and study tours that foster their learning of linguistics.

these activities.
[a]  |  If the course is not taken to fulfill requirement 1(a), it may

course.
[c]  |  Students are required to take PSYC2010. Linguistics and
Psychology Double Major Programme students who have taken any of the
equivalent statistics courses including COMM3110,DOTE2011, ECON2121, GRMD2102, LING3403, PHPC2015, SEEM2430,
SOCI2004, SPED2600, STAT2001 and 2006, STAT2101 and 2102 are exempted
from taking PSYC2010, and they are required to take another PSYC course
at 2000 or above listed under 3(b) Elective Courses to compensate for the
units.

Minor Programme Title Linguistics
Minor Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 18 units of
courses, with at least 6 units of courses at 3000 or above level, as
follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Course:  |  3
LING1000
2.  |  Elective Courses:
(a)  |  Any
2 courses from the following:  |  6
LING2003,
2004, 2005, 2006, 3101
(b)  |  LING3201
or 3204  |  3
(c)  |  2 LING courses excluding LING3405, 3406, 4000 and 4001[a]  |  6
Total:  |  18
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  This
Minor Programme is not applicable to students who major in Bimodal
Bilingual Studies, and Linguistics, or students who double major in
Linguistics and Translation, and Linguistics and Psychology.
[a]  |  Including courses listed under 2(a)
and 2(b) not taken to fulfill the requirement of 2(a) or 2(b).
2.  |  No more than 9 units of courses
taken to fulfill the requirements of the students’ Major Programme(s) and
other Minor Programme(s) respectively can be used to fulfill the
requirement of this Minor Programme.
